## ChatGPT

Yes—but **catalogued and dormant** is the correct state, not “installed and running.”

The registry should distinguish:

```text
catalogued
installed_dormant
authentication_pending
jit_leased
active
degraded
disabled
rejected
```

A dormant server should have:

- An official endpoint or pinned local package.
- Documented authentication and required environment-variable names.
- A captured tool inventory.
- Empty default tool exposure.
- No active capability lease.
- No direct IDE entry.
- A tested activation and rollback procedure.
- No background process unless the implementation requires one.

## Important corrections to the list

Some names represent the same capability, while others are frameworks rather than MCP servers.

| Requested item | Correct treatment |
|---|---|
| GitHub | Keep the official GitHub MCP server dormant. |
| GitHub Actions | Use the `actions` toolset inside GitHub MCP; do not create another server. GitHub’s official server already covers workflow runs and CI/CD. citeturn937876search0turn937876search6 |
| GitLab | Add the official GitLab remote MCP as dormant. It is currently Beta. citeturn296135search1turn296135search3 |
| Obsidian | Already registered and currently active; do not duplicate it. Convert its tool exposure to dormant/JIT. fileciteturn39file0L94-L147 |
| GitLens | Register **GitKraken MCP**, which is the actual server installed through GitLens or GitKraken CLI. citeturn300933search0turn300933search2 |
| Firecrawl | Add official Firecrawl MCP as dormant; classify scraped content as untrusted. citeturn937876search1 |
| Google Sheets | Add Google’s official Sheets MCP, currently Developer Preview, dormant with read/write tools separated. citeturn626826search0turn626826search1 |
| Google Super | I am mapping this to **Google Workspace Universal Search MCP**, the closest official current service. It searches Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and Chat through one read-only search tool. citeturn626826search6 |
| YouTube for developers | Google currently documents YouTube APIs, but I did not find an official Google YouTube MCP server. Keep this as an unverified candidate, not an installed community server. citeturn932706search1turn932706search2 |
| Dropbox | I did not find an official Dropbox MCP in Dropbox’s developer sources. Keep it as `candidate_unverified` until an official or strongly validated provider is selected. |
| Cloudflare | Add the official Cloudflare API MCP and documentation MCP. Do not add every product-specific Cloudflare server initially. The API server reduces the full API to `search` and `execute`. citeturn158271search0 |
| AgentMail | Add the official hosted AgentMail MCP dormant; sending and deletion tools require explicit approval. citeturn158271search3 |
| Context7 | Add as a dormant fallback behind Arabold, not as the primary docs source. Context7’s official remote endpoint is available to MCP clients. citeturn861580search10turn861580search17 |
| Vercel | Add Vercel’s official OAuth MCP dormant. citeturn861580search2 |
| CrewAI | CrewAI is an agent/workflow framework, not a cloud-service MCP server to expose globally. Keep it in the dependency/template catalog. citeturn295031search5 |
| Microsoft AutoGen | AutoGen is also an agent framework and MCP client through `McpWorkbench`; do not register AutoGen itself as an upstream server. citeturn861580search1turn861580search4 |
| LangGraph | LangGraph is already the AgentCore workflow authority. Add only the official **LangChain documentation MCP** at `https://docs.langchain.com/mcp`. citeturn435314search0 |
| MCP Servers | The official registry/reference repository is a discovery source, not one runtime server. Reference implementations are explicitly not automatically production-ready. citeturn336561search0turn336561search1 |
| Agno | Add Agno’s official documentation MCP at `https://docs.agno.com/mcp`; do not add the entire Agno framework as a Bifrost server. citeturn459776search2 |
| File System Server | Already registered and currently active, bounded to `D:\github`. Do not duplicate it; convert it to dormant/project-scoped JIT exposure. fileciteturn39file0L193-L238 |

The current registry already lists GitHub MCP as deferred/disabled, while Obsidian and Filesystem are active. fileciteturn30file0L121-L134 Context7 is currently forbidden by the canonical policy, so your new instruction requires changing it from **forbidden** to **secondary dormant fallback**. fileciteturn29file0L144-L153

## Recommended dormant catalog

### Read-only documentation and discovery

```text
context7
langchain-docs
agno-docs
cloudflare-docs
openrouter
mcp-registry-reference
google-workspace-universal-search
```

### Repository and engineering operations

```text
github
gitlab
gitkraken
filesystem
vercel
cloudflare-api
```

### External content and data

```text
firecrawl
google-sheets
agentmail
obsidian
```

### Candidate—not installed yet

```text
youtube-developer
dropbox
crewai-runtime
autogen-runtime
```

## Activation policy

```text
Read-only documentation:
    JIT activation may be automatic for the current task.

Sensitive read access:
    Requires an approved project/profile lease.

Write-capable service:
    Requires operator approval before the lease.

Destructive or externally visible action:
    Requires per-action confirmation.

Unknown/community MCP:
    Cannot activate until security and provenance review passes.
```

Only one overlapping write surface should be active at a time. For example:

- Do not activate GitHub MCP write tools and GitKraken GitHub write tools simultaneously without a specific need.
- Do not activate Filesystem and broad GitKraken file-changing operations for unrelated repositories.
- Keep Context7 off when Arabold is sufficient.
- Keep Cloudflare API off when only Cloudflare documentation is needed.

# Cursor prompt

```text
BUILD THE AGENTCORE DORMANT MCP CAPABILITY CATALOG

Use BLUEPRINT.md as locked architecture authority.

Canonical repository:

    D:\github\agentcore-control-plane

Expected current baseline:

    fc3fb16 or later

Inspect actual current main HEAD before making changes.

Goal:

Register, prepare, validate, and document the MCP capabilities we may need across all
managed non-Swarm IDEs while leaving them dormant by default.

All IDEs continue to use exactly one entry:

    agentcore-gateway
    http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp

Do not add these servers directly to individual IDE configurations.

Do not expose all tools merely because a server is registered.

Do not change any IDE's default model/provider.

Preserve the OpenRouter rule:

- OpenRouter is optional for all IDEs except Open Interpreter.
- OpenRouter MCP is not a default inference route.
- Open Interpreter may use OpenRouter as its default API provider separately.

Do not touch Swarm.

===============================================================================
1. DORMANT CAPABILITY MODEL
===============================================================================

Use or extend the existing tool lifecycle without creating a second policy system.

Required lifecycle states or equivalent existing states:

    catalogued
    installed_dormant
    authentication_pending
    jit_leased
    active
    degraded
    disabled
    rejected

A dormant MCP server must have:

- canonical ID;
- official provider/owner;
- pinned package version or official remote endpoint;
- transport;
- authentication type;
- environment-variable names only;
- OAuth/scopes requirements;
- exact tool inventory from live or controlled discovery;
- tool risk classification;
- capability-profile eligibility;
- empty default tool exposure;
- no active JIT lease;
- health test;
- activation procedure;
- deactivation procedure;
- rollback route;
- documentation source;
- discovery timestamp;
- security review state.

Do not use wildcard tool grants.

For local stdio servers, do not leave unnecessary processes running.

For remote servers, registry presence does not imply authentication or connection.

===============================================================================
2. RECONCILE EXISTING ENTRIES
===============================================================================

Inspect the current Bifrost registry and live rendered configuration.

Existing known cases:

- github-mcp is already deferred/disabled;
- obsidian-vault already exists;
- filesystem already exists;
- Context7 is currently forbidden;
- OpenRouter may be under separate implementation.

Do not create duplicates.

Convert Obsidian and Filesystem to dormant/JIT exposure without uninstalling them or
breaking their configuration.

Change Context7 from forbidden to:

    secondary
    dormant
    fallback_only

Context7 may activate only when:

- Arabold cannot answer;
- exact-version documentation is unavailable;
- independent corroboration is required;
- the operator explicitly requests Context7.

Arabold remains primary.

===============================================================================
3. APPROVED DORMANT MCP UPSTREAMS
===============================================================================

Register or reconcile the following.

A. GitHub

Official server:

    GitHub MCP Server

Prefer the current official remote OAuth server when compatible with Bifrost.

Do not create a separate GitHub Actions server.

Use GitHub toolsets, including:

    context
    repos
    issues
    pull_requests
    actions
    code_security
    dependabot
    secret_protection

Keep write-capable tools dormant and operator-gated.

B. GitLab

Official remote endpoint for GitLab.com:

    https://gitlab.com/api/v4/mcp

Status:

    beta

Use OAuth.

Prefix tools to avoid GitHub collisions.

Read tools may be JIT-leased.
Issue, merge-request, pipeline, branch, and repository mutations require operator
approval.

C. GitKraken MCP

This is the correct server for GitLens/GitKraken capabilities.

Do not create a separate `gitlens` server.

Prefer the GitKraken CLI installation path where it provides one reusable Bifrost
upstream.

Keep GitHub/GitLab provider operations from GitKraken dormant when the official
provider MCP is active.

Local Git status/history/read tools may receive a narrower lease than remote write
tools.

D. Firecrawl

Use the official Firecrawl MCP implementation.

Store:

    FIRECRAWL_API_KEY

as a Windows User environment-variable name only.

Classify all scraped/search/crawled content as:

    raw_untrusted

It may not enter global curated memory automatically.

Crawls, batch extraction, deep research, and paid/high-volume calls require bounded
leases and cost controls.

E. Google Sheets

Official endpoint:

    https://sheetsmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1

Status:

    Developer Preview

Authentication:

    OAuth 2.0

Separate tools into:

Read:

    get_values
    get_spreadsheet

Write:

    update_spreadsheet
    update_values
    update_formulas
    insert_dimension

Read access is sensitive and project-scoped.

Write access requires operator approval and per-action confirmation.

F. Google Workspace Universal Search

Treat the operator phrase `Google Super` as this entry unless a different exact product
is later specified.

Official endpoint:

    https://workspacemcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1

Tool:

    search_corpus

Status:

    Developer Preview

Use read-only OAuth scopes.

Results may contain private email, Drive, Calendar, and Chat data.

Do not write returned content into global memory without explicit promotion.

G. Cloudflare

Register only these initial official servers:

    cloudflare-api
    cloudflare-docs

Endpoints:

    https://mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp
    https://docs.mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp

Do not initially add every Cloudflare product-specific MCP server.

cloudflare-docs:

    read-only documentation profile

cloudflare-api:

    operator/JIT only
    search may be read-only
    execute is write-capable and requires operator approval

Use OAuth or a least-privilege Cloudflare token according to current official Bifrost
support.

H. AgentMail

Official endpoint:

    https://mcp.agentmail.to/mcp

Prefer OAuth through Bifrost where supported.

Separate tools:

Read:

    inbox/thread/message/list/get operations

Write:

    create inbox
    draft
    send
    reply
    attachment upload
    update labels

Destructive:

    delete inbox/message/draft or equivalent

Sending and deletion require per-action confirmation.

I. Context7

Official endpoint:

    https://mcp.context7.com/mcp

Use only as an Arabold fallback.

Read-only.

No automatic use merely because it exists.

Use CONTEXT7_API_KEY from Windows User environment variables only when required for
rate limits or private sources.

J. Vercel

Official endpoint:

    https://mcp.vercel.com

Authentication:

    OAuth

Status:

    Beta

Separate:

Read:

    documentation
    teams
    projects
    deployments
    logs

Write:

    project/deployment/domain/environment changes

Write tools require operator approval.

K. LangChain/LangGraph documentation

Official documentation MCP:

    https://docs.langchain.com/mcp

Canonical ID:

    langchain-docs

Purpose:

    current LangChain, LangGraph, LangSmith, and Deep Agents documentation

This does not replace the existing AgentCore LangGraph workflow.

This is read-only and dormant unless LangGraph ecosystem work is active.

L. Agno documentation

Official documentation MCP:

    https://docs.agno.com/mcp

Canonical ID:

    agno-docs

Do not register the Agno framework itself as an MCP service.

This is a read-only documentation server.

M. Filesystem

Reuse the existing filesystem server.

Do not create another entry.

Keep it dormant by default.

Activation must be project-scoped to the currently registered worktree.

Never expose:

    C:\
    D:\
    E:\
    F:\
    G:\
    H:\
    I:\

as whole-drive roots.

The active allowed root must be the assigned project/worktree only.

N. Obsidian

Reuse the current obsidian-vault server.

Keep it dormant by default.

Read and search may use a docs-knowledge lease.

Writes require operator approval.

Do not permit direct filesystem writes to the active vault.

===============================================================================
4. CATALOG-ONLY ITEMS
===============================================================================

Do not configure the following as live Bifrost MCP upstreams.

CrewAI:

- framework/runtime;
- keep in dependency catalog, templates, docs, and reference implementations;
- may consume MCP but is not itself a required shared MCP service.

Microsoft AutoGen:

- framework and MCP client through McpWorkbench;
- keep as an approved/under-review dependency and reference implementation;
- do not register the framework itself as a global MCP server.

LangGraph:

- already AgentCore's workflow/checkpoint authority;
- do not create a second LangGraph server;
- use langchain-docs for current documentation.

Model Context Protocol Servers:

- use the official MCP Registry and reference-server repository as discovery and
  evaluation sources;
- do not register the entire registry as one MCP upstream;
- reference servers are not automatically production-approved.

===============================================================================
5. UNVERIFIED CANDIDATES
===============================================================================

Create catalog records only, with:

    status: candidate_unverified

for:

    youtube-developer
    dropbox

Do not install a random community server.

For YouTube:

- record official YouTube Data API, Analytics API, Reporting API, and OAuth docs;
- determine the exact desired use:
  - developer documentation;
  - video search;
  - metadata;
  - transcripts;
  - channel management;
  - uploads;
- do not add automation that violates YouTube API policies;
- later choose an official server if Google releases one or build a minimal governed
  adapter only when there is a real use case.

For Dropbox:

- search current official Dropbox developer sources;
- if no official MCP exists, evaluate either:
  - a vetted partner;
  - a minimal first-party Dropbox API adapter;
- no community server enters the approved catalog without security review.

===============================================================================
6. CAPABILITY GROUPS
===============================================================================

Create named capability groups equivalent to:

docs-read:

    arabold-docs
    context7
    langchain-docs
    agno-docs
    cloudflare-docs

repository-read:

    github read tools
    gitlab read tools
    gitkraken local read tools

repository-write:

    GitHub/GitLab/GitKraken branch, issue, PR, merge, push, and workflow mutation

web-research:

    firecrawl search/scrape

web-crawl-heavy:

    firecrawl crawl, batch, deep research

workspace-private-read:

    google-workspace-universal-search
    google-sheets read
    obsidian read

workspace-write:

    google-sheets write
    obsidian write

deployment-read:

    vercel read
    cloudflare read

deployment-write:

    vercel write
    cloudflare execute

mail-read:

    agentmail inbox/thread/message reads

mail-write:

    agentmail draft/send/reply

destructive-external:

    deletes
    production deployment mutation
    DNS changes
    domain changes
    repository destructive actions

No group is active globally by default.

===============================================================================
7. ACTIVATION RULES
===============================================================================

Read-only documentation tools:

- may receive short automatic JIT leases when directly relevant;
- revoke at task or Milestone close.

Sensitive read tools:

- require project scope and an explicit task purpose.

Write tools:

- require operator approval before lease creation.

Destructive or externally visible actions:

- require per-call confirmation.

Paid tools:

- require spending and invocation bounds.

Overlapping providers:

- do not activate duplicate write surfaces simultaneously unless explicitly justified.

Every activation records:

- project
- IDE/client
- agent
- session/run/thread
- server
- exact tools
- reason
- start
- expiry
- approval class
- invocation/cost bounds
- revocation result

===============================================================================
8. AUTHENTICATION POSTURE
===============================================================================

Do not authenticate every dormant server merely to call it installed.

Use these readiness states:

    ready_no_auth_required
    ready_auth_on_first_use
    authenticated_dormant
    blocked_missing_account
    blocked_missing_official_server

OAuth authorization should occur on first real activation unless frequent use justifies
preauthorization.

Never commit:

- OAuth tokens;
- refresh tokens;
- API keys;
- client secrets;
- query-string credentials;
- cookies;
- resolved secret-bearing configuration.

Use Windows User-scope environment variables and Bifrost's supported secure OAuth
storage.

Report environment-variable names only.

===============================================================================
9. RENDERING AND IDE BEHAVIOR
===============================================================================

All integrations live behind Bifrost.

Do not edit any live IDE MCP config.

Every IDE continues to contain only:

    agentcore-gateway

Newly enrolled IDEs inherit the dormant catalog automatically.

A dormant server or tool must not appear in the model's active tool context.

Activation must refresh Bifrost tool discovery without requiring duplicate IDE entries.

Preserve:

- existing models;
- default providers;
- context-window settings;
- authentication;
- sandbox/profile settings;
- OpenRouter optional-model policy.

===============================================================================
10. VALIDATION
===============================================================================

For each approved server:

- validate official provenance;
- validate endpoint/package;
- validate transport;
- capture tool inventory;
- validate auth mechanism;
- run one safe read-only smoke when possible;
- validate empty default exposure;
- create and revoke one bounded test lease;
- prove tool disappearance after revocation;
- prove no IDE config changed;
- prove no secret was committed or logged;
- verify overlapping server policy;
- document rollback.

For OAuth servers that require operator interaction:

- stop only for the unavoidable consent step;
- report scopes and account before authorization.

Run:

- registry and schema validation;
- renderer tests;
- capability-profile tests;
- lifecycle/JIT tests;
- Bifrost restart/reconnect;
- central durability audit;
- exact ten agentcore-memory tool invariant;
- project-router invariant;
- IDE renderer parity;
- secret scan;
- Swarm exclusion.

===============================================================================
11. DOCUMENTATION
===============================================================================

Create or update one canonical catalog/runbook such as:

    docs\operations\DORMANT_MCP_CAPABILITY_CATALOG.md

For each entry include:

- status;
- official source;
- purpose;
- endpoint/package;
- auth;
- tool groups;
- risk;
- activation;
- deactivation;
- rollback;
- ownership;
- overlap notes;
- last verification.

Update:

    contracts\bifrost-upstream-mcp-registry.json
    contracts\project-tool-lifecycle.json
    contracts\global-agent-policy.yaml
    MASTER_CONFIG_AND_PROMPT.md
    docs\bifrost\CAPABILITY_PROFILES.md
    docs\bifrost\MCP_CLASSIFICATION_MATRIX.md
    .agentcore\docs\DOCS_INDEX.md
    ide-profiles\*\GLOBAL_RULES.md
    ide-profiles\*\VALIDATION.md

Do not create duplicate setup instructions.

===============================================================================
12. COMPLETION REPORT
===============================================================================

Report:

- starting and final commits;
- approved dormant servers;
- existing entries reconciled;
- catalog-only frameworks;
- unverified candidates;
- exact official endpoints/packages;
- authentication readiness per server;
- captured tool counts;
- capability groups;
- JIT policy;
- overlapping-server protections;
- servers with operator OAuth still pending;
- MASTER_CONFIG SHA-256;
- validation matrix;
- confirmation all new tools are dormant by default;
- confirmation no live IDE config changed;
- confirmation default IDE models/providers are unchanged;
- confirmation ten-tool AgentCore memory surface unchanged;
- confirmation Swarm untouched.

Use final status:

    DORMANT MCP CAPABILITY CATALOG READY

only when all approved entries are registered, documented, testable, and expose zero
tools without a lease.
```

This gives you a large ready-to-use capability library without loading hundreds of tools into every model context or leaving write-capable services continuously available.

## ChatGPT

Do **not** send the previous prompt yet. It should include an extension-to-MCP audit, but we should not assume that MCP servers can replace most Cursor extensions.

## Core distinction

- **MCP servers give the agent capabilities.**
- **Extensions give the editor language support, diagnostics, debugging, formatting, UI panels, syntax highlighting, remote environments, and test explorers.**

Most extensions in the screenshots should remain installed. Dormant MCP servers add agent capabilities without loading tools into active context, but they do not replace editor functionality.

## Extensions that MCP can substantially replace

| Installed extension | Replacement | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub Pull Requests | Official GitHub MCP | Removable after GitHub MCP passes PR/issue/repository tests, unless you use the native PR sidebar |
| GitHub Actions | GitHub MCP `actions` toolset | Removable after workflow-run/log/failure tests pass |
| Obsidian for Cursor | Existing `obsidian-vault` MCP | Removable if you do not need the embedded Obsidian preview/navigation interface |
| Hostinger Connector | Official Hostinger MCP behind Bifrost | Strong candidate for removal after central OAuth and tool validation |
| Giga AI Context Manager | AgentCore memory, STATE, Context Fabric and project context | Strong candidate for removal; no additional MCP needed |

The official GitHub MCP includes repositories, issues, pull requests, Actions, security, Dependabot and other individually selectable toolsets. This is exactly the type of integration that can replace agent-facing portions of the two GitHub extensions. citeturn204087search1

The current AgentCore registry already contains Obsidian, Playwright and Filesystem upstreams, so these should be reconciled rather than duplicated. fileciteturn39file0L94-L147 fileciteturn39file0L149-L238

Hostinger Connector is itself an MCP bootstrap extension. It can register Hostinger directly into Cursor, Claude Code and Codex, which conflicts with the AgentCore rule that every IDE has only `agentcore-gateway`. The official Hostinger MCP should instead be registered once behind Bifrost. After that is authenticated and validated, the extension can be disconnected and removed. citeturn826591search1

Giga analyzes repositories, generates `SPEC.md`, and writes project context into `.cursorrules`. AgentCore now owns this job through PostgreSQL, generated STATE projections, Context Fabric and continuous capture, so Giga is overlapping infrastructure. citeturn826591search5

## Partial replacements

| Installed extension | MCP/CLI alternative | What would be lost by uninstalling |
|---|---|---|
| Playwright Test for VS Code | Existing Playwright MCP plus Playwright CLI/skills | Test Explorer, breakpoint/debug UI, editor test controls |
| SQLite Viewer | Project-scoped database MCP | Human visual table browser |
| Database Client / JDBC | Project database MCP | Connection tree, SQL editor and human result-grid UI |
| Docker / Container Tools | Possible Docker APIs/MCP | Container sidebar, logs UI, Compose UI and editor integration |
| AtomClaw / openclaw magnet | OpenClaw’s own interfaces | Cursor/OpenClaw visual management |

Microsoft’s Playwright project says CLI plus skills is often more token-efficient for coding agents, while MCP is useful for persistent browser state and iterative automation. Neither replaces the VS Code Test Explorer and debugger UI. citeturn204087search0

Do **not** give normal agents broad database MCP access to replace Database Client. AgentCore deliberately prevents direct PostgreSQL credentials and raw SQL access. A future project-database MCP could be dormant and restricted to an application’s development database, but never the canonical AgentCore database. fileciteturn31file0L40-L43

AtomClaw and the magnet connector belong to OpenClaw’s independent ecosystem. The magnet extension mainly manages the OpenClaw connection/status, while AtomClaw is an alternative OpenClaw client and management UI. They should not be replaced through the non-Swarm AgentCore gateway. citeturn826591search9turn826591search10

## Extensions MCP cannot replace

These should remain when you use their associated language or workflow:

```text
Dev Containers
Container Tools
Docker
Remote - SSH
WSL
Expo Tools
Python by Anysphere
Python by Microsoft
Python Debugger
Ruff
ESLint
Prettier
PowerShell
Tailwind CSS IntelliSense
YAML
i18n Ally
EditorConfig
markdownlint
Rainbow CSV
SQLite Viewer
Database Client / JDBC
```

They provide editor-hosted services that MCP does not reproduce:

- Language servers
- IntelliSense and completion
- Type checking
- Inline diagnostics
- Debug adapters
- Format-on-save
- Syntax and schema validation
- Remote/container workspaces
- Test Explorer
- Editor decorations

### Python extensions

The three visible Python components are not automatically duplicates:

- Anysphere Python is Cursor’s Pyright/type-checking implementation.
- Microsoft Python supplies Python commands and runtime integration.
- Python Debugger supplies `debugpy`.

Cursor reports that Cursor Pyright and Microsoft Python can serve different roles. The main extension to avoid alongside Cursor Pyright is **Pylance**, because simultaneous activation can conflict. citeturn152540search4turn152540search12

Do not remove either visible Python extension until Cursor tests:

```text
interpreter selection
Run Python File
Run Selection/Line
imports and autocomplete
type diagnostics
debugging
Ruff integration
virtual-environment detection
```

## Cleanup candidates that do not need MCP replacements

These are convenience or visual extensions. Cursor should compare them against current built-in functionality and your actual use:

```text
Enlighten for Cursor
Material Icon Theme
Better Comments
Color Highlight
Auto Rename Tag
Highlight Matching Tag
Path Intellisense
TODO Highlight
Todo Tree
Turbo Console Log
File Utils
ES7+ React snippets
Import Cost
Pretty TypeScript Errors
Code Spell Checker
DotENV
Markdown All in One
```

Likely overlaps:

- Keep either `TODO Highlight` or `Todo Tree` if one is enough.
- Auto Rename Tag and Highlight Matching Tag may overlap built-in HTML/tag behavior.
- Path Intellisense may overlap current path completions.
- Color Highlight and Better Comments are presentation only.
- ES7 snippets are less valuable when agents generate components.
- Turbo Console Log is less valuable when agents insert diagnostics.
- Markdown All in One and markdownlint are complementary, but much of Markdown All in One may be optional.
- Material Icon Theme has no development capability.
- Enlighten is training content, not infrastructure.

## Important extension-specific decisions

### Hostinger Connector

Add Hostinger to the dormant MCP catalog. The extension should not remain responsible for writing MCP entries to several IDEs.

Correct flow:

```text
Cursor/Codex/other IDE
    → agentcore-gateway
    → Bifrost
    → official Hostinger MCP
```

Hostinger says its connector dynamically exposes more than 100 account-management tools and all calls affect the live account because there is no sandbox. Hostinger write operations must therefore stay operator-gated. citeturn826591search1turn826591search2

### Giga AI Context Manager

This should probably be removed after Cursor verifies that no project depends on:

```text
SPEC.md generated by Giga
.gigaignore
Giga-managed .cursorrules content
```

AgentCore now provides a stronger project-centric implementation:

```text
PostgreSQL canonical history
.agentcore/STATE.md
.agentcore/DECISIONS.md
.agentcore/CONTEXT_INDEX.md
Context Fabric drift
startup_context
retrieve_context
exact source expansion
```

### OpenClaw extensions

Because OpenClaw/ClawX remain independent, do not have AgentCore uninstall them automatically. Classify:

```text
openclaw magnet connector
AtomClaw
```

as `Swarm/OpenClaw client UI`. Keep whichever one you actively use; remove duplicates only through a separate Swarm-aware decision.

# Add this section to the dormant MCP prompt

```text
===============================================================================
EXTENSION-TO-MCP SUBSTITUTION AND CURSOR CLEANUP AUDIT
===============================================================================

Before installing or enabling additional MCP capabilities, audit the complete installed
Cursor extension set.

Do not uninstall any extension during the initial audit.

The purpose is to identify extensions whose agent-facing capabilities are fully or
partially replaced by MCP servers behind agentcore-gateway, while preserving essential
editor, language, debugger, formatting, remote-workspace and UI functionality.

1. INVENTORY

Capture for every installed extension:

- exact extension ID
- display name
- publisher
- version
- enabled/disabled status
- workspace/global scope
- activation time
- approximate disk footprint
- dependencies and extension-pack relationships
- project recommendation source, if any
- whether it writes MCP config
- whether it stores credentials
- whether it starts a background process
- whether it is actively used in the current repositories

Use Cursor's extension inventory and Developer: Show Running Extensions.

Do not infer duplication from similar names alone.

2. CLASSIFICATION

Classify each extension as exactly one primary category:

    editor_essential
    language_server
    debugger
    formatter_linter
    remote_environment
    test_ui
    data_ui
    mcp_bootstrap_extension
    agent_context_extension
    optional_editor_convenience
    duplicate_or_conflicting
    swarm_openclaw_only
    unused_unverified

Also record:

    mcp_fully_replaces_agent_capability
    mcp_partially_replaces_agent_capability
    cli_skill_replaces_agent_capability
    no_replacement

3. EXPECTED REPLACEMENT CANDIDATES

GitHub Pull Requests:
- agent capability replaced by official GitHub MCP pull_requests/issues/repos toolsets;
- native sidebar and review UI are not replaced.

GitHub Actions:
- agent capability replaced by official GitHub MCP actions toolset;
- native workflow-tree UI is not replaced.

Obsidian for Cursor:
- agent read/search/write capability replaced by the existing obsidian-vault MCP;
- embedded preview/navigation UI is not replaced.

Hostinger Connector:
- it is an MCP bootstrap extension;
- register official Hostinger MCP once behind Bifrost;
- do not allow the extension to create direct Cursor, Claude or Codex MCP entries;
- after central OAuth and read/write/JIT validation, disconnect it cleanly before
  uninstalling;
- Hostinger write tools remain operator-gated because they affect live infrastructure.

Giga AI Context Manager:
- compare its SPEC.md/.cursorrules behavior against AgentCore memory, generated STATE,
  Context Fabric and project bootstrap;
- preserve any unique accepted project information in canonical AgentCore memory;
- classify as removable when AgentCore fully supersedes it.

Playwright Test for VS Code:
- Playwright MCP and CLI/skills replace agent browser automation;
- they do not replace Test Explorer, trace viewer or interactive debugging;
- classify removal according to operator UI usage.

SQLite Viewer / Database Client / Database Client JDBC:
- do not replace with unrestricted database MCP access;
- AgentCore PostgreSQL remains inaccessible to normal agents;
- a future application-database MCP must be project-scoped, dormant and separately
  approved;
- keep human database UI when used.

AtomClaw and openclaw magnet:
- classify as Swarm/OpenClaw-only client integrations;
- do not route them through AgentCore;
- do not uninstall them in this task;
- identify functional duplication and report it for a separate operator decision.

4. EXTENSIONS THAT MUST NOT BE REPLACED BY MCP

Preserve required functionality for:

- Dev Containers
- Container Tools
- Docker
- Remote SSH
- WSL
- Expo
- Python runtime integration
- Python type checking
- Python debugging
- Ruff
- ESLint
- Prettier
- PowerShell
- Tailwind
- YAML
- i18n
- EditorConfig
- markdownlint
- CSV/table visualization
- test and debugger UIs

MCP tools may complement these extensions but are not substitutes.

5. PYTHON CONFLICT AUDIT

Inspect:

    Anysphere Python / Cursor Pyright
    ms-python.python
    ms-python.debugpy
    Pylance, if present

Verify:

- type checking
- completion
- interpreter selection
- virtual environments
- Run Python File
- Run Selection/Line
- debugging
- Ruff integration

Do not remove the Anysphere Python, Microsoft Python or Python Debugger extension merely
because multiple Python entries appear.

Prevent simultaneous conflicting Pylance and Cursor Pyright activation.

6. OPTIONAL CONVENIENCE CLEANUP

Evaluate current built-in Cursor support and actual usage for:

- Enlighten for Cursor
- Material Icon Theme
- Better Comments
- Color Highlight
- Auto Rename Tag
- Highlight Matching Tag
- Path Intellisense
- TODO Highlight
- Todo Tree
- Turbo Console Log
- File Utils
- ES7 snippets
- Import Cost
- Pretty TypeScript Errors
- Code Spell Checker
- DotENV
- Markdown All in One

Do not claim MCP replaces these.

For overlaps such as TODO Highlight versus Todo Tree, recommend one preferred extension
based on actual use.

7. SAFE REMOVAL GATE

An extension may be recommended for removal only when:

- its exact replacement capability is identified;
- the official MCP/CLI/AgentCore path is installed or registered;
- the replacement is dormant by default where appropriate;
- authentication is validated;
- one bounded functional test passes;
- unique UI functionality is documented;
- settings and extension inventory are backed up;
- no dependency requires it;
- no project recommends it as mandatory;
- rollback/reinstall command is recorded.

Do not uninstall during this audit.

Produce:

    audits/CURSOR_EXTENSION_TO_MCP_REPLACEMENT_MATRIX.md

The matrix must include:

- extension
- exact ID/version
- current purpose
- replacement
- full/partial/no replacement
- unique UI lost
- dependency risk
- credentials/config behavior
- recommendation
- removal readiness
- rollback

8. UPDATE THE DORMANT MCP CATALOG

Ensure the dormant catalog includes or reconciles:

- github
- obsidian-vault
- playwright
- hostinger
- filesystem

Do not create duplicates.

Hostinger must be added as an official dormant MCP integration with:

- official package/endpoint
- OAuth readiness
- empty default exposure
- read/write/destructive tool groups
- operator approval for live infrastructure mutations
- no direct per-IDE registration

9. COMPLETION REPORT

Report:

- total installed extensions
- active/running extension count
- editor-essential count
- full MCP replacement candidates
- partial MCP replacement candidates
- convenience cleanup candidates
- duplicate/conflict candidates
- Swarm/OpenClaw-only extensions
- estimated extension reduction after approved removals
- exact MCP servers required before removal
- extensions requiring separate operator approval
- confirmation no extension was uninstalled
- confirmation no live IDE config changed
- confirmation no default model/provider changed
- confirmation Swarm was untouched
```

## Expected initial result

Likely removable after validation:

```text
GitHub Pull Requests
GitHub Actions
Hostinger Connector
Obsidian for Cursor
Giga AI Context Manager
```

Potentially removable depending on whether you use their UI:

```text
Playwright Test for VS Code
SQLite Viewer
Database Client / JDBC
many convenience/decorative extensions
```

Keep the core language, debugger, formatter, remote-development and container extensions. Removing those in favor of MCP would make Cursor less capable, not more capable.

## ChatGPT

## Assessment

**Do not execute this plan unchanged.** The core architecture is right, but it conflicts with your newer dormant/JIT tool policy in several places.

The plan correctly preserves:

- One IDE entry: `agentcore-gateway`
- No direct OpenRouter entries in each IDE
- The ten-tool `agentcore-memory` surface
- OAuth at the Bifrost layer
- Separation between OpenRouter MCP and OpenRouter model inference
- Operator gating for billable tools
- Swarm isolation. fileciteturn3file0

OpenRouter officially uses a remote OAuth flow that creates a dedicated seven-day key with a configurable $10 default cap. The MCP is a development assistant, not a replacement for OpenRouter’s normal API/provider integration. citeturn382399search1

## Required corrections

### 1. OpenRouter must be dormant by default

The plan currently proposes:

```text
enabled: true
status: active
```

and gives eleven tools permanently to builder, reviewer, and docs profiles. That contradicts the goal of keeping tools out of active context until needed.

Use:

```text
server runtime: registered and optionally connected
default tools_to_execute: []
lifecycle: installed_dormant or ready_auth_on_first_use
```

Then lease the read-only discovery group only when an agent needs OpenRouter information.

Bifrost supports deny-by-default virtual keys, tool groups, per-tool execution controls, and disabled MCP clients, so this dormant design fits the gateway. citeturn382399search6

### 2. Do not create a second tool-policy mechanism

`profile_tool_overrides` must not become an independently maintained policy layer.

The canonical path is already:

```text
PostgreSQL capability profile / JIT lease
    → AgentCore Tool Lifecycle Manager
    → Bifrost tools_to_execute
```

Any registry representation must be derived from that policy, not manually duplicated. The blueprint makes PostgreSQL-backed capability profiles and leases authoritative. fileciteturn0file5

### 3. The proposed `denied_tools` fix is incomplete

This logic still fails when permitted tools are:

```json
["*"]
```

because it preserves the wildcard and therefore cannot enforce named denials.

Required rule:

- Explicit allowlists may subtract denied tools.
- `["*"]` plus named denials must fail validation, or the renderer must first resolve the exact discovered inventory and generate an explicit allowlist.
- No wildcard grants for OpenRouter.

### 4. JIT activation must use the actual lease system

The plan proposes proving JIT by temporarily editing the operator VK tool list. That only proves config mutation.

The acceptance test must:

1. Create a PostgreSQL-backed capability lease.
2. Let the Tool Lifecycle Manager apply the change.
3. Confirm the tool appears.
4. Revoke or expire the lease.
5. Confirm the tool disappears.
6. Confirm restart reconciliation preserves the correct state.

No manual VK editing as the production mechanism.

### 5. Separate Open Interpreter provider configuration

The OpenRouter MCP integration and this change are separate:

```text
Open Interpreter → OpenRouter API as default inference provider
```

Do not bundle a live Open Interpreter config edit into the MCP gateway implementation.

After the MCP integration passes, perform a separate bounded Open Interpreter task that:

- Finds the actual active configuration path and schema.
- Backs it up.
- Preserves all non-model settings.
- Uses `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` without printing it.
- Selects an exact verified model ID.
- Confirms every other IDE retained its current default provider.

### 6. Correct the backup location

The plan uses:

```text
E:\AgentCore-Backups\
```

The canonical model uses:

```text
E:\AgentCoreArchive\agentcore-memory\backups\
G:\AgentCoreArchive\agentcore-memory\backups\
```

Use the established E: backup hierarchy and create the G: mirror. fileciteturn0file2

### 7. Do not assume `config.db` encryption

Cursor should prove from matching Bifrost source or documentation that OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest.

Until proven, classify:

```text
H:\AgentRuntime\bifrost\data\config.db
```

as secret-bearing and protect its ACLs and backups accordingly. Do not state “encrypted” merely because the token is stored in SQLite.

### 8. Remove the invented fallback environment name

Do not add:

```text
OPENROUTER_MCP_KEY
```

to secret scanning or documentation unless the implementation actually uses that environment variable. The selected design uses OAuth.

### 9. Remove mutable hardcoded counts

Do not validate that “127 existing tools remain.”

Use:

- A pre-change sanitized tool-inventory snapshot
- Expected server prefixes
- Exact protected surfaces, especially the ten memory tools
- A comparison showing no existing capability disappeared unexpectedly

The current total can change as the dormant catalog grows.

### 10. Use MCP health checks, not HTTP `HEAD`

A Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint may reject ordinary `HEAD` or unauthenticated `GET`.

Use:

- Bifrost MCP client state
- OAuth state
- MCP `initialize`
- `notifications/initialized`
- `tools/list`
- One safe read-only call

## Important scope clarification

This Cursor plan covers **OpenRouter only**. It does not implement the larger dormant catalog for GitHub, GitLab, Firecrawl, Sheets, Vercel, Cloudflare, AgentMail, Context7, and the other requested capabilities. It also does not perform the extension-to-MCP replacement audit.

Those remain a separate task after OpenRouter proves the dormant/JIT pattern.

## Paste this into Cursor Plan mode

```text
CORRECT THE OPENROUTER PLAN BEFORE EXECUTION

Keep the verified endpoint, live tool discovery, OAuth research, single-gateway
architecture, documentation work, safety boundaries, and validation approach.

Apply these binding corrections:

1. OpenRouter must be registered but dormant by default.

   Do not use:
       enabled=true + status=active + permanent builder/reviewer tool exposure

   Use the current lifecycle equivalents of:
       installed_dormant
       ready_auth_on_first_use
       authenticated_dormant

   Default tools_to_execute for every normal profile must be empty.

2. Create named OpenRouter tool groups:

   openrouter-discovery-read
   openrouter-account
   openrouter-billable

   Discovery tools receive short task-scoped JIT leases.
   Account tools require operator scope.
   Billable tools require operator approval, cost bounds, invocation bounds, expiry,
   and per-action confirmation.

3. Do not create a competing profile policy.

   PostgreSQL-backed AgentCore capability profiles and leases remain canonical.

   Any Bifrost profile/tool rendering must be derived from the existing Tool Lifecycle
   Manager and contracts.

4. JIT validation must use a real AgentCore capability lease.

   Do not prove JIT by directly editing the operator VK.

5. Fix denied_tools correctly.

   Reject wildcard permitted_tools combined with named denied_tools unless the exact
   discovered inventory is materialized into an explicit allowlist.

   Add a deterministic validator.

6. Use the established backup hierarchy:

       E:\AgentCoreArchive\agentcore-memory\backups\bifrost\
       G:\AgentCoreArchive\agentcore-memory\backups\bifrost\

   Register the backup locations in the resource-location model.

7. Do not state that Bifrost config.db encrypts OAuth tokens unless matching source or
   official documentation proves it.

   Until proven, treat config.db and its backups as secret-bearing.

8. Do not add OPENROUTER_MCP_KEY unless an implemented fallback actually consumes it.

9. Replace hardcoded total tool counts such as 127 with a sanitized before/after
   inventory comparison and invariant checks.

10. Use MCP initialize/tools-list/safe-call and Bifrost client status for health.
    Do not depend on HTTP HEAD against the MCP endpoint.

11. Separate Open Interpreter model-provider configuration from this gateway task.

    The OpenRouter MCP task must not edit Open Interpreter’s live config.

    Produce a follow-up bounded task for:
       Open Interpreter → OpenRouter API default provider

    Preserve every other IDE’s current provider/model configuration.

12. OAuth posture:

    Prefer ready_auth_on_first_use when the server is rarely used, avoiding mandatory
    weekly reauthorization for a dormant capability.

    authenticated_dormant is acceptable only when the operator chooses the seven-day
    reauthorization burden.

13. Final OpenRouter state:

    - registered once behind Bifrost
    - zero tools exposed without a lease
    - no direct IDE entries
    - no default IDE model changes
    - no paid inference during validation
    - ten-tool agentcore-memory surface unchanged
    - Swarm untouched

Revise the plan and present the corrected final plan for approval.

Do not execute yet.
```

After Cursor returns the corrected plan, it should be safe to leave Plan mode and execute.

### What is complete

## What “dormant” means

OpenRouter is **prepared but not currently usable**.

```text
Registered in AgentCore: yes
Present as a Bifrost upstream: yes
Authenticated: no
Visible tools in IDEs: no
Paid tools available: no
Default model changed: no
```

When OpenRouter information is actually needed:

1. Complete the one-time browser OAuth approval.
2. Set a low spending cap.
3. Create a short `openrouter-discovery-read` capability lease.
4. Use the read-only tools.
5. Let the lease expire or revoke it.

Account tools and billable inference require their stricter approval paths.

There is no reason to activate OAuth now merely to prove the dormant registration.
